console.summary: No browser console messages or JavaScript errors observed during reviewed flows.

review.strongest_part: Immigration Scenario Lab transparency: assumptions, confidence labels, myth testing, cause-and-effect map and winners/losers.
review.largest_obstacle: The dashboard End Year/year progression workflow is not self-explanatory and can appear stuck.
review.likely_first_time_completion: Partial: users can adjust controls and see some results, but may not understand annual progression without finding Reports controls.
review.highest_risk_abandonment_point: After changing a lever and pressing End Year on the dashboard, because the UI enacted values but still displayed Year 2020 / Turn 1 of 5 and a second End Year click did not visibly advance.

scorecard.explanation: Purpose is visible, but the first-time workflow and turn state need clearer guidance.
Major sections are visible, but simulation, review and year progression controls are split and confusing.
Rich content, but too much is exposed at once, especially in Reports and Immigration.
Sliders have labels, ranges and explanatory text; some units and assumptions need clearer immediate feedback.
Pending/enacted state exists, but End Year/year progression feedback is weak and delayed text updates can confuse.
Reports, baseline comparison and narrative explanations are useful once found.
Strong caveats, assumptions, confidence labels, myth tester and cause map.
Dashboard score and cards are clear; detail pages are dense and some sticky/header behavior competes with content.
Buttons/sliders are exposed and slider keyboard input works; focus visibility and long accessible names need review.
Narrow layout stacks well and controls remain readable; header consumes too much vertical space.
No console errors observed; primary reliability concern is unclear/possibly inconsistent End Year behavior.

findings.title: Primary End Year workflow does not clearly advance the simulation
evidence: After changing Immigration Rate to 2.6% and pressing top END YEAR, the status changed to ENACTED RESULTS and calculations updated.
The header remained Year 2020 / Turn 1 of 5.
Pressing END YEAR again produced no visible change in the snapshot.
In Reports, Step Forward changed the header to Year 2021 / Turn 2 of 5 and the title to Year 2 cabinet briefing papers.
findings.why_it_matters: The primary dashboard CTA appears to be the central simulation action. If it does not visibly advance time or explain what happened, users may think the app is broken.
findings.recommended_change: After End Year, show an explicit completion state and a single obvious next action such as Advance to Year 2021, or combine enact/advance into one clear flow.
findings.expected_improvement: More users complete the core simulation loop and understand annual progression.

findings.title: Slider explanatory text can disagree with the changed value before enactment
evidence: After changing Immigration Rate from 2.0% to 2.6%, the panel displayed 2.6% but still said immigration adds about 200,000 people and creates demand for 80,000 homes.
After End Year, the text updated to 260,000 people and 104,000 homes.
findings.why_it_matters: Users read explanatory text immediately after moving a slider. If values disagree, trust drops.
findings.recommended_change: Split pending estimate and enacted result, or update explanatory text immediately with a label such as If enacted.
findings.expected_improvement: Stronger trust and clearer cause/effect feedback.

findings.title: First-time workflow lacks a guided path from setting levers to reading results
evidence: Dashboard exposes eight sections, six adjustment cards, two End Year buttons and multiple score cards.
Baseline comparison and winners/losers are not introduced on the dashboard.
findings.why_it_matters: A new user can see many controls but may not know the intended sequence.
findings.recommended_change: Add a compact three-step simulation path: choose/adjust policies, enact year, review baseline and winners/losers. Highlight the next action after each step.
findings.expected_improvement: Lower abandonment and faster first successful run.

findings.title: Reports and Immigration pages are powerful but overwhelming
evidence: Reports includes department cards, turn controls, score, weights, baseline comparison, factors, narratives and many expandable panels.
Immigration includes glossary, scenario presets, controls, annual result, winners/losers, myth tester, cause map, assumption ledger, comparison table, timeline and entity foundation.
findings.why_it_matters: Rich transparency can become unusable if users cannot find the most relevant next insight.
findings.recommended_change: Add an executive summary / What changed most / Who gained and lost section before deep detail, with jump links to assumptions, winners/losers and baseline.
findings.expected_improvement: Users get value before deciding whether to inspect detail.

findings.title: Mobile/narrow layout gives too much space to header chrome
evidence: In the narrow view, navigation, year card and full-width End Year button occupied a large part of the first screen before the briefing and score.
Content required substantial scrolling.
findings.why_it_matters: Casual mobile users need the first screen to explain the task quickly.
findings.recommended_change: Collapse navigation into a menu or horizontal scroll, make year/turn status compact, and keep the primary CTA sticky only after the user has made a change.
findings.expected_improvement: More useful content appears above the fold and mobile users orient faster.

findings.title: Sticky/header behavior can obscure content during detailed policy editing
evidence: Visual inspection after opening Government Policy Decisions on desktop showed the sticky top header overlaying the policy panel area while scrolled into controls.
findings.why_it_matters: Obscured controls reduce confidence and make editing feel fragile.
findings.recommended_change: Reserve layout space for sticky headers or reduce their height in edit panels; ensure anchor targets scroll below the header.
findings.expected_improvement: Smoother detailed editing with less visual interference.

findings.title: Baseline comparison is valuable but under-signposted from the dashboard
evidence: Baseline Comparison appeared in Reports with useful deltas: Economic Growth +0.2, Fairness -0.6, Government Finances +2.4, Environment Score -0.7, Housing Stress -2.3.
The dashboard did not clearly direct users there after End Year.
findings.why_it_matters: Without baseline, users see scores but not whether their policy package improved or worsened the scenario.
findings.recommended_change: After End Year, show a small dashboard summary of top changes vs baseline and a link to full baseline comparison.
findings.expected_improvement: Better comprehension of consequences and trade-offs.

findings.title: Environment metric naming risks interpretation errors
user_journeys: Interpret overall and individual outcome scores
evidence: Dashboard card says Environment 58.8 and Environmental pressure is lower-is-better.
Reports later refer to Environment Score, where higher appears better because it is calculated as 100 minus environmental pressure.
findings.why_it_matters: Users may not know whether a higher number is good or bad.
findings.recommended_change: Use consistent naming or add visible higher/lower-is-better direction markers everywhere.

findings.title: Keyboard focus visibility is not strong enough
evidence: Sliders responded to keyboard input.
After pressing Tab twice the visible focus state was not strongly obvious in visual inspection.
findings.why_it_matters: Keyboard users need to know where they are before changing a policy lever.
findings.recommended_change: Add a high-contrast focus ring for buttons, cards, sliders and disclosure controls.
findings.expected_improvement: Better accessibility and lower accidental input risk.

findings.title: Some card buttons have long accessible names
evidence: Snapshot exposed long button names such as Current Path plus its full description.
Dashboard adjustment cards include label, value, status and Adjust details in the button name.
findings.why_it_matters: Long repeated labels slow screen reader navigation.
findings.recommended_change: Give card buttons concise aria-labels and keep longer explanatory text associated separately.

top_five_improvements: Fix or clarify the End Year / advance year workflow.
Update slider consequence explanations immediately or label them as pending.
Add a first-time guided path on the dashboard.
Create an executive results summary before deep reports.
quick_wins: Change the dashboard instruction text to explain enacted vs pending results and what End Year does.
Add a high-contrast focus style for all buttons, slider thumbs and disclosure controls.
Rename or direction-label environment metrics consistently so users know whether higher or lower is better.

suggested_acceptance_tests.test: Start on Dashboard, change Immigration Rate, press End Year, and confirm the UI displays what happened plus an obvious next action to advance or review the next year.
Change Immigration Rate and verify every displayed consequence either updates immediately or is clearly labelled as the previous enacted result.
Ask a new user to run one simulation and find who benefited and who bore costs; pass if they reach baseline comparison and winners/losers without help.
Open in a phone/narrow viewport and verify purpose, year/turn state and one clear next action are visible without excessive header domination.
Navigate dashboard and policy panel using only keyboard; pass if focus is always visible and sliders can be adjusted intentionally.
